name: Quadrifilar Helical (wire)
description: A quadrifilar helical antenna consists of four helical wire elements wound around a cylindrical support structure, typically fed in quadrature phase to produce circular polarization. This antenna type provides excellent circular polarization purity and hemispherical coverage patterns. It is widely used in satellite communications, GPS applications, and mobile communications where circular polarization and broad beamwidth are required.

helix_diameter.name: Helix Diameter
helix_diameter.description: Diameter of the cylindrical helix structure

helix_length.name: Helix Length
helix_length.description: Axial length of each helical element

pitch_angle.name: Pitch Angle
pitch_angle.description: Angle between helix axis and wire element

turns.name: Number of Turns
turns.description: Number of helical turns per element

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining helix geometry for desired frequency, calculating wire dimensions, optimizing for circular polarization, and implementing quadrature feed network.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency
steps.description: Establish center frequency and bandwidth requirements

steps.title: Calculate Helix Diameter
steps.description: Set diameter for proper circumference relative to wavelength

steps.title: Determine Helix Length
steps.description: Calculate axial length for quarter-wave resonance

steps.title: Calculate Pitch Angle
steps.description: Determine pitch angle for optimal radiation characteristics

steps.title: Size Wire Elements
steps.description: Select wire radius for mechanical stability and electrical performance

steps.title: Design Feed Network
steps.description: Create 90-degree hybrid network for quadrature excitation

steps.title: Optimize Axial Ratio
steps.description: Adjust element spacing and phasing for circular polarization

equations.gain: G = 10 * log10(15 * (D / lambda)^2 * L / lambda * sin^2(alpha))
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta, phi) = (sin(theta) / (1 + cos(theta))) * exp(j * n * phi)

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use method of moments with wire segments and junction analysis for four-element helical structure
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply helical antenna theory with quadrature excitation and pattern multiplication for four elements

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', Chapter 9
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', Chapter 8
Kraus, J.D. 'Antennas for All Applications', Chapter 7
